persona.name: Jasmine Taylor

occupation.title: Student / Cross-Country Runner
occupation.description: Sophomore majoring in Exercise Science. Runs Division II cross-country and outdoor track (5K, 10K). Full athletic scholarship covers tuition and meals. Picks up tutoring shifts for spending money.

persona.education: Currently pursuing B.S. in Exercise Science at NC Central. Graduated valedictorian from T.C. Roberson High School in Asheville. Plans to pursue a Doctor of Physical Therapy degree.
long_term_goals: Earn All-Conference in cross-country before graduating
Get into a top DPT program (Emory or Duke)
Keep her shoes under $100 per pair while competing at a high level
Help her younger sister navigate college applications
persona.style: Jasmine is direct and competitive — she doesn't waste words or money. She calculates cost-per-mile when evaluating shoes and knows exactly how many miles each pair has on them. She speaks with confidence built from being the fastest woman on her team but doesn't brag — performance speaks. She's practical to the point of austerity: owns 3 pairs of shoes total (daily trainer, racing flat, recovery) and rotates them by plan. Her communication is clear, slightly formal from academic writing, and laced with quiet determination.

traits: Budget-conscious out of necessity, not frugality
Disciplined about training, sleep, nutrition, and spending
Calculates value in everything — cost per mile, cost per wear
Protective of her scholarship and academic standing
Trusts her coach's shoe recommendations but verifies independently

interests: Running shoe durability and value analysis
Meal prepping on a student budget
Trail runs in Duke Forest on easy days
likes: Brands with student or team discount programs
Lightweight trainers that can double as race-day shoes
Teammates who share shoe recommendations honestly
dislikes: Shoes over $150 that don't last proportionally longer
Being marketed to as if she has disposable income
Carbon-plate shoes that cost more than her monthly food budget
Brands that don't offer team or student pricing
Heavy shoes that slow her down
Trendy shoes that sacrifice durability for aesthetics
People who assume she can afford whatever they recommend

beliefs: Hard work and discipline matter more than expensive gear
A $90 shoe that lasts 500 miles beats a $170 shoe that lasts 300
Running is her pathway out — she won't waste the opportunity
Education first, athletics second — the scholarship is a means
skills: Distance running race strategy and pacing
Tutoring and explaining complex concepts simply

general: Tracks shoe mileage meticulously in a notes app
Buys previous model-year shoes on Running Warehouse clearance
Asks her coach before buying any new shoe
Shares shoe reviews with teammates via group text

workday: Lunch in dining hall (scholarship meal plan)
Tutoring shift 3-5 PM or study hall
Second run if it's a doubles day
evening: Ice bath or foam rolling in the training room
weekend: Saturday: race day or long run (10-14 miles)
Team recovery: pool session or easy jog

health.physical: Excellent fitness. Lean and strong. History of a stress reaction in her left tibia sophomore year of high school — resolved fully but she's careful about mileage increases. Iron levels occasionally low; takes supplements. Sees the team athletic trainer weekly for preventive care.
health.mental: Resilient and focused. Some pressure about maintaining scholarship standards (3.0 GPA minimum). Misses her family in Asheville. Manages stress through routine and faith. Occasionally anxious about post-graduation finances.

relationships.name: Denise Taylor
relationships.description: Postal worker in Asheville. Single mom who raised Jasmine and her sister. Jasmine calls every night — Denise is her anchor.

relationships.name: Kayla Taylor
relationships.description: High school junior, also runs track. Jasmine is helping her with college applications and SAT prep.

relationships.name: Coach Linda Harris
relationships.description: Former Olympic Trials qualifier. Strict but fair. Jasmine trusts her training plans and shoe advice completely.

relationships.name: Bria Washington
relationships.description: Distance runner from Charlotte. They share shoe reviews and split bulk orders for running supplies.
